if I'm in a directory and run pc archeologist and I select multiple paths, will the conversations from those paths will converge into the wiki of the current PWD?
No — the archeologist routes each transcript to the wiki of the project that conversation was in, not the current PWD.
let's instead allow the user to select multiple directories and they all merge into the current project (for when sessions exist in different paths for the same project)
Changed so all selected sessions in the interactive picker route to the current PWD's wiki; capture markers are global so merged sessions won't later populate their original path's wiki.
if I'm running from /Users/pablofernandez/Work/serial-killer you could put at the top and pre-selected the conversations that match in the list
Now the picker floats matching projects to the top and pre-checks them, with all selections writing to the current directory's wiki.

what I see in pc archeologist's window is utterly useless... I would like to see "I looked at conversation 123", "I discovered this fact", "Wrote guide abc", etc, etc. This needs a rethink of this UI to make it human-compatible, not a log of a bunch of bullshit
The advisor is right — I need to verify the data before building the UI. Two key checks first.

Completed the TUI redesign: added date, session_id, and text excerpts to event payloads in capture.rs, and rewrote the archeologist feed with natural-language lines plus a navigable detail pane (Enter to open, Esc to close).
